title: Heddal (village)

is a village in [Notodden Municipality](/wiki/Notodden_Municipality "Notodden Municipality") in [Telemark](/wiki/Telemark "Telemark") county, [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). The village is located in the Heddalen valley, along the river [Heddøla](/wiki/Hedd%C3%B8la "Heddøla"), about 7 kilometres (4.3 mi) to the east of the town of [Notodden](/wiki/Notodden_(town) "Notodden (town)"), about 3 kilometres (1.9 mi) northwest of the village of [Yli](/wiki/Yli "Yli"), and about 13 kilometres (8.1 mi) to the southeast of the village of [Sauland](/wiki/Sauland "Sauland") (in [Hjartdal Municipality](/wiki/Hjartdal_Municipality "Hjartdal Municipality")). The [European route E134](/wiki/European_route_E134 "European route E134") highway runs through the village area.

**Heddal** is a village in [Notodden Municipality](/wiki/Notodden_Municipality "Notodden Municipality") in [Telemark](/wiki/Telemark "Telemark") county, [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). The village is located in the Heddalen valley, along the river [Heddøla](/wiki/Hedd%C3%B8la "Heddøla"), about 7 kilometres (4.3 mi) to the east of the town of [Notodden](/wiki/Notodden_(town) "Notodden (town)"), about 3 kilometres (1.9 mi) northwest of the village of [Yli](/wiki/Yli "Yli"), and about 13 kilometres (8.1 mi) to the southeast of the village of [Sauland](/wiki/Sauland "Sauland") (in [Hjartdal Municipality](/wiki/Hjartdal_Municipality "Hjartdal Municipality")). The [European route E134](/wiki/European_route_E134 "European route E134") highway runs through the village area.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9a/Heddal_og_Notodden_Museumslag-1.jpg/250px-Heddal_og_Notodden_Museumslag-1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Heddal_og_Notodden_Museumslag-1.jpg)

View of the local museum

The village is fairly rural with many farms spread throughout it. The historic [Heddal Stave Church](/wiki/Heddal_Stave_Church "Heddal Stave Church") and the [Heddal Open Air Museum](/wiki/Heddal_Open_Air_Museum "Heddal Open Air Museum") are both located in the village, drawing many tourists to the area each year. The village was the [administrative centre](/wiki/Administrative_centre "Administrative centre") of the old [Heddal Municipality](/wiki/Heddal "Heddal") which existed from 1838 until 1964.[[2]](#cite_note-snl-2)
